items.title: New Bitcoin quantum proposal offers Satoshi Nakamoto a way to prove control without moving BTC
items.desc: A new design proposed by venture fund Paradigm would let holders privately timestamp proof that they control vulnerable keys before quantum computers arrive, creating a possible rescue path if Bitcoin ever sunsets old addresses.

items.title: Paradigm researcher proposes timestamp ‘escape hatch’ to protect Satoshi-era bitcoin from quantum threats
items.desc: PACTs would let long-term bitcoin holders prepare for a worst-case quantum scenario without moving funds or signaling activity onchain.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation sells another 10,000 ETH to BitMine in third OTC deal
items.desc: The Ethereum Foundation has now sold roughly $47 million worth of ETH to BitMine in a week, drawing fresh criticism over the pace and scale of its sales.

items.title: Bitcoin above $78,000 as Senate clears Clarity Act yield hurdle, S&P 500 sets new record
items.desc: Bitcoin recovered from a midweek dip to $75,500 to climb back above $78,000 by Saturday morning in Asia, with the Senate's stablecoin yield compromise removing a key roadblock to crypto market structure legislation.

items.title: Bitcoin doesn’t need a fresh narrative to reclaim $100K: Analyst
items.desc: With attention spilling into multiple other technology sectors, crypto may struggle to capture a strong, price-driving narrative, a crypto analyst says.

items.title: CLARITY Act stablecoin yield rules finalized: ‘Go time’ for crypto bill
items.desc: Galaxy Digital head of research Alex Thorn expects the banking industry to “increase their opposition efforts” following the release of the final stablecoin yield provisions.

items.title: Bitcoin Optech Newsletter #403
items.desc: This week’s newsletter describes research around using binary fuse filters as an alternative to the GCS used in compact block filters. Also included are our regular sections summarizing proposals and discussion about changing Bitcoin’s consensus rules, announcing new releases and release candidates, and describing notable changes to popular Bitcoin infrastructure software.

items.title: Clarity Act text lets crypto firms offer stablecoin rewards while shielding bank yield
items.desc: The text released Friday blocks crypto firms from offering stablecoin yield offerings that look like bank deposits, but "bona fide" transactions are allowed.

items.title: Three Bitcoin data points suggest a rally to $80K is imminent
items.desc: Bitcoin chases $80,000 as rising spot volumes and futures open interest suggest the market has shifted back in the bulls’ favor.

items.title: A16z backs CFTC, says state-by-state rules on prediction markets create ‘barrier to impartial access’
items.desc: Venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz argued that state prediction market restrictions could drain liquidity and limit market access.

items.title: JPMorgan says rising stablecoin use may not lead to similar market cap growth
items.desc: Stablecoin transaction volume is rising fast, but higher velocity may limit how much total market cap grows, JPMorgan analysts said.

items.title: From NYSE Gut Punch to ‘One App for Money’: Exodus Bets Self‑Custody Can Power Everyday Life
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine From NYSE Gut Punch to ‘One App for Money’: Exodus Bets Self‑Custody Can Power Everyday Life Exodus used its hometown summit in Omaha to argue that self-custody is moving from niche crypto tool to full-stack payments infrastructure, even as the company navigates regulatory scars and a softer market. This post From NYSE Gut Punch to ‘One App for Money’: Exodus Bets Self‑Custody Can Power Everyday Life first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Aave Deposits on MegaETH Cross $575M as Post-TGE Liquidity Pours In
items.desc: MegaETH’s DeFi TVL has doubled since Thursday’s MEGA token launch, with USDM and Terminal Points farming pulling in fresh capital.

items.title: XRP sentiment hits 2-year high, but why is price stuck?
items.desc: XRP social media sentiment has turned bullish following integration with Rakuten Wallet, but resistance at $1.40 could cap the upside.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation’s recent ETH sales to Tom Lee’s Bitmine hit $47 million after latest deal
items.desc: EF said the funds will go towards operations, activities, protocol R&D, ecosystem development, community grant funding and more.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Sells $23 Million More in ETH to Tom Lee's BitMine
items.desc: For the second straight week, the Ethereum Foundation has unloaded 10,000 ETH—about $23 million worth—to top treasury firm, BitMine.

items.title: Bitcoin Pushes Above $78,000 as Risk Assets Shake Off Hawkish Fed
items.desc: Crypto markets open May with a rally despite an unresolved Hormuz blockade.

items.title: Exodus (EXOD) Announces Official UFC Deal and Revised, Self-Custody Money App
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Exodus (EXOD) Announces Official UFC Deal and Revised, Self-Custody Money App In front of shareholders and customers in Omaha, Exodus dived deep into Exodus Pay and announced a high-profile UFC sponsorship. This post Exodus (EXOD) Announces Official UFC Deal and Revised, Self-Custody Money App first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation finalizes sale of 10,000 ether to BitMine as part of its treasury strategy
items.desc: The latest transaction follows a similar March deal in which the foundation sold 5,000 ETH to BitMine, raising roughly $10.2 million.

items.title: Galoy Pushes Deeper Into U.S. Banking With All-in-One Bitcoin Platform
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Galoy Pushes Deeper Into U.S. Banking With All-in-One Bitcoin Platform Galoy is rolling out an expanded Bitcoin-native banking platform aimed at helping U.S. banks and credit unions integrate lending, payments, and custody without overhauling their core systems. This post Galoy Pushes Deeper Into U.S. Banking With All-in-One Bitcoin Platform first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Canadian pension giant AIMCo buys the dip in Strategy, now sitting on $69 million unrealized gain
items.desc: AIMCo returns to Michael Saylor's bitcoin treasury company years after exiting, now sitting on a sizable unrealized gain.

items.title: A new narrative for bitcoin that will last
items.desc: Of the myriad pundits proclaiming what bitcoin is or isn’t, Blume offers a more clear-eyed framing that, he argues, will outlast the others.

items.title: European crypto asset manager CoinShares records $7.4 billion AUM in first annual filing since Nasdaq listing
items.desc: CoinShare saw revenue of $126.4 million on its asset management business in 2025, up 13% from the $111.7 million the year prior.

items.title: Tether posts $1.04 billion Q1 profit, reaches $8.23 billion reserve buffer
items.desc: The stablecoin issuer said the crypto market was highly volatile during the first quarter.

items.title: Ethereum ETFs Shed $184M Over 4-Day Negative Streak
items.desc: Outflows from Ethereum ETFs extended to a 4-day streak as Bitcoin funds shed $490 million, even as the S&P 500 hit an all-time high.

items.title: Tether Reports Billion-Dollar Q1 Profit Amid Crypto Slump—And Says Audit Has Begun
items.desc: Stablecoin giant Tether says it holds over $141 billion in U.S. Treasuries, a figure that has never been verified by a full audit.

items.title: WisdomTree’s crypto ETPs reported net inflows of $137 million in Q1
items.desc: Cumulatively, assets under management for the firm's crypto ETPs rose to roughly $1.8 billion during Q1 of this year.

items.title: Crypto onramping solution Fun raises $72 million Series A co-led by Multicoin Capital and SignalFire
items.desc: Fun powers deposits, withdrawals, and settlement flows for some of crypto’s leading apps, including Polymarket, Lighter, and Aave.

items.title: Dogecoin Hits 2-Month High as DOGE Mining Firm Plans to Go Public via Merger
items.desc: Nasdaq-listed pharmaceutical company Shuttle pivots to crypto mining in first major institutional play for Dogecoin mining.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: DeFi can freeze stolen funds, but not everyone agrees it should
items.desc: Crypto faces backlash for freezing stolen funds and for doing nothing, with expectations pulling in opposite directions.

items.title: Crypto VC funding plunges to $659M in April, hits near two-year low
items.desc: Crypto VC funding fell to $659 million in April, its lowest monthly total since July 2024, as dealmaking slowed across the sector.

items.title: Institutional demand to drive bitcoin market cap to $16 trillion by 2030: Ark Invest
items.desc: The figure implies a sharp increase in the bitcoin price.

items.title: Senators Warren, Wyden Quiz Commerce Secretary Lutnick Over Tether Loan to Children’s Trust
items.desc: The probe follows reports that Dynasty Trust A, which benefits Lutnick’s children, borrowed an undisclosed sum from the stablecoin issuer.

items.title: Tether posts over $1 billion Q1 profit as reserve buffer reaches record $8.2 billion
items.desc: Tether's approximate $141 billion exposure to U.S. Treasurys positions it as one of the largest holders globally.

items.title: Brazil central bank prohibits crypto use in regulated cross-border payments under new FX rules
items.desc: The rule forms part of the central bank's broader effort to bring cross-border payments fully inside the regulated FX system.

items.title: Bitcoin bounces as big tech earnings fuel optimism; short-term pressures remain
items.desc: Your day-ahead look for May 1, 2026

items.title: SBI Holdings eyes stake in crypto exchange Bitbank to build digital asset powerhouse
items.desc: The Tokyo-based broker is betting big on crypto with plans in Singapore and a Visa partnership for bank cards that allows users to accumulate digital assets.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs draw $2B in April for highest monthly inflows this year
items.desc: US spot Bitcoin ETFs posted strong April inflows as Bitcoin rallied, with IBIT leading gains despite late-month outflows across funds.

items.title: SBI Holdings in talks to acquire stake in crypto exchange Bitbank, eyes subsidiary status
items.desc: The talks are at an early stage, pending due diligence and internal procedures, with more details on the acquisition timing to come.

items.title: South Korean Court Lifts Bithumb's Six-Month Business Suspension
items.desc: A Seoul court has reversed March sanctions that would have restricted the exchange's core functions over alleged compliance failures.

items.title: Bitcoin ticks higher, but remains range-bound as traders keep short bias
items.desc: BTC rises to $77,000 after holding $75,000 support, but negative funding, unchanged open interest and cautious positioning signal a lack of conviction.

items.title: Brazil bars crypto settlement in regulated cross-border payment rails
items.desc: Brazil’s central bank barred virtual assets from settlement inside regulated eFX payment rails as it tightens oversight of crypto-linked flows.

items.title: SBI eyes Bitbank deal as Japan’s crypto exchange market consolidates
items.desc: SBI Holdings is in discussions to make Bitbank a subsidiary, adding to its push to acquire crypto exchanges amid improving regulatory clarity in Japan.

items.title: South Korean court grants stay on Bithumb’s six-month suspension pending final ruling: reports
items.desc: South Korea's financial watchdog issued the suspension in March, alongside a 36.8 billion won ($25 million) fine.

items.title: Bitcoin edges above $77,000, but institutional activity suggests downside hedging
items.desc: BTC rises on steady volume and strong technical structure, but surging put interest and muted prediction market odds point to defensive positioning.

items.title: Bitcoin seals best monthly gain in a year as S&P 500 hits fresh all-time high
items.desc: Bitcoin finished April above $76,000 to preserve most of its monthly gains, but the S&P 500 stole the limelight with a trip back to record highs.

items.title: Riot reports $33 million in first data center revenue as AMD doubles contracted capacity
items.desc: Most of Riot Platforms' data center revenue came from lower-margin fit-out work, while recurring leases played a smaller role.

items.title: Bakkt completes acquisition of stablecoin payments firm Distributed Technologies Research
items.desc: Bakkt announced the deal in January, which was originally for 9.3 million shares, along with a corporate name change to Bakkt Inc.

items.title: Bithumb scores a legal win in South Korea as six-month suspension is lifted by local judge
items.desc: South Korea's financial watchdog imposed a $24.6 million fine on Bithumb and partial suspension that came into effect last month.

items.title: Tether reports $1.04B profit in Q1 as Treasury holdings reach $141B
items.desc: The top stablecoin issuer’s balance sheet remains heavily concentrated in US Treasuries as stablecoin adoption expands across emerging markets.

items.title: Bitcoiners launch 'The Bitcoin Evidence Base' to stamp out FUD
items.desc: The Bitcoin FUD-stopping tool cites over 22 peer-reviewed research papers to address common misconceptions about Bitcoin.

items.title: DeFi protocol Carrot becomes first casualty of $285M Drift exploit
items.desc: Carrot's total value locked has collapsed 93% in a month, from $28 million to $1.99 million, leaving the protocol financially unable to continue.

items.title: Bitcoin’s surge to $77K pressures shorts, but absent spot and long leverage caps rallies
items.desc: Bitcoin bulls took another swing at the $77,000 resistance, but profit-taking and traders’ reluctance to increase margin and spot longs limit the strength of each breakout.

items.title: Tokenized RWA market grows 420% since 2025 on regulatory clarity, access
items.desc: Tokenized US Treasurys were one of the biggest growth areas of the RWA market, rising from a market capitalization of $3.9 billion at the start of 2025 to more than $15 billion.

items.title: US Senate bans itself from betting on prediction markets
items.desc: The US Senate unanimously passed a rule banning members and staff from prediction markets, with a similar resolution set to be introduced in the House.

items.title: Repeated Bitcoin profit taking near $77K suggests rally is losing steam
items.desc: Short-term traders took profit each time BTC rallied above $77,000, creating overhead sell pressure that has capped BTC’s ability to reach $80,000.

items.title: U.S. senators won't be weighing in on prediction markets bets after banning themselves
items.desc: The Senate agreed unanimously to revise its rules to ban members and their staffs from wagers on prediction markets platforms.

items.title: Stablecoins overtake Bitcoin in Latin America crypto purchases: Bitso
items.desc: A Bitso report shows shifting user behavior as dollar-linked stablecoins gain traction for everyday financial use across Latin America’s inflation-hit economies.

items.title: Arbitrum DAO starts vote to release 30,766 frozen ETH to DeFi United, following Kelp DAO attack
items.desc: The Arbitrum Security Council froze 30,766 ETH that the Kelp DAO attacker had moved to an Arbitrum One address.

items.title: Bitcoin Crash Incoming? April Surge Was Built on Shaky Ground, Analysts Warn
items.desc: Analysts warn that the Bitcoin rally was fueled by speculative futures trading, mirroring conditions that preceded the 2022 crypto crash.

items.title: Crypto Markets Catch a Breath After Three-Day Slide
items.desc: Bitcoin reclaims $76,000 despite U.S. spot ETFs posting a third consecutive day of outflows.

items.title: Crypto hacks hit record high in April as exploits kept piling up
items.desc: With the end of April in sight, another exploit involving dormant Ethereum mainnet addresses appears to have occurred.

items.title: From Cathie Wood to Cantor Fitzgerald, the big money is betting that Robinhood’s crypto slump is just a temporary speed bump
items.desc: Cathie Wood’s Ark Invest snapped up over 500,000 shares of Robinhood on Wednesday, joining a wave of analysts who believe a surge in April trading activity will outweigh a recent earnings disappointment.

items.title: Senate Votes to Ban Senators and Staff From Using Prediction Markets
items.desc: S. Res. 708 prohibits senators and staff from using prediction markets, and became effective immediately upon passage.

items.title: MegaETH Token Debuts at $2 Billion Valuation
items.desc: MEGA is live on Binance, Coinbase, and 11 other venues a week after the Ethereum Layer 2 network cleared its first performance milestone.

items.title: Anchorage Digital Partners with M0 on US Stablecoin Issuance Stack
items.desc: The partnership aims to make it easier for companies, including fintechs and paying firms, to issue compliant stablecoins in the United States.

items.title: North Korean Hackers Have Stolen $6 Billion in Crypto—Including 76% of 2026's Spoils: TRM
items.desc: Pyongyang-linked hackers drained $577 million from two DeFi platforms in April, underscoring an escalating threat to crypto markets.

items.title: Polymarket Taps Chainalysis to Police Insider Trading
items.desc: The prediction market will deploy a custom on-chain detection model to flag insider activity.

items.title: Senate moves to ban themselves from prediction market trading amid insider concerns
items.desc: U.S. senators are now barred from trading on prediction markets following the unanimous passage of a resolution on Thursday.

items.title: Coinbase's Institutional Investment Arm Taps Superstate to Launch Tokenized Credit Fund
items.desc: Coinbase Asset Management has selected Superstate FundOS to issue on-chain shares of Coinbase Stablecoin Yield Fund (CUSHY).

items.title: Senators Warren, Wyden press Tether and Commerce Secretary Lutnick over loan to family trust
items.desc: Sens. Warren and Wyden are pressing Tether and Commerce Secretary Lutnick over a reported loan made to a trust tied to Lutnick’s children.

items.title: PayPal Elevates Crypto to Core Business in Strategic Reorganization
items.desc: The payments giant will fold PYUSD, Braintree, and merchant processing into a new Payment Services & Crypto division, marking the first time digital assets have a dedicated home within the company.

items.title: Coinbase’s ‘CUSHY’ stablecoin fund to launch tokenized share class via Superstate in Q2
items.desc: The Coinbase Stablecoin Yield Fund aims to generate yield by lending stablecoins and private credit investment opportunities.

items.title: Crypto Is the Most Muted Topic on Elon Musk’s X, Ahead of Politics: Product Head
items.desc: Internal data shows users mute crypto more than politics and global conflict on X’s For You feed.

items.title: Polymarket Launches Market Integrity Tools Following Soldier’s Insider Trading Arrest
items.desc: The team-up between Polymarket and Chainalysis aims to bring institutional-grade compliance monitoring to crypto prediction markets.

items.title: Senator Warren questions Commerce Secretary Lutnick on Tether loan to family
items.desc: Senators Elizabeth Warren and Ron Wyden sent letters to Howard Lutnick and Tether CEO Paulo Ardoino asking about a loan Tether reportedly made to Lutnick's family.

items.title: MegaETH Token Launches as Ethereum Layer-2 Network's Novel Rewards System Is Tested
items.desc: Ethereum layer-2 network MegaETH will unlock its native MEGA token over time, and reward it to users, as key benchmarks are met.

items.title: Twenty-One Weighs Mergers With Strike, Elektron to Create Publicly Traded Bitcoin Giant
items.desc: Tether Investments proposed a three-way merger to create a Bitcoin platform combining mining, treasury management, and financial services

items.title: Wasabi Loses $5M+ in Latest DeFi Exploit
items.desc: Wasabi Protocol is the latest victim in what appears to be a record bad month for DeFi hacks.

items.title: A Polymarket-linked bet on the weather in France forecasts a major data issue
items.desc: The incident shows that as more real-world outcomes become tradable, the real bottleneck is not trading itself, but the integrity and certification of the data used for settlement, argues Hallali.

items.title: Polymarket taps Chainalysis to bring Wall Street-level oversight to crypto prediction markets
items.desc: By partnering with Chainalysis to monitor its blockchain data in real-time, Polymarket is signaling to both users and regulators that it is serious about eliminating insider trading and market manipulation.

items.title: Gemini Gains Key CFTC Approval to Expand Prediction Market, Perps Offerings
items.desc: A DCO license will now allow crypto exchange Gemini to act as a clearinghouse for its derivatives-related businesses.

items.title: Crypto for Advisors: Breaking down the Sui blockchain
items.desc: Sui is a differentiated Layer-1 blockchain, combining novel object-based architecture and parallel execution for high throughput. It's optimized for consumer Web3 apps.

items.title: Maple Finance's SYRUP Token Now Available on Revolut in UK and EU
items.desc: Maple Finance's SYRUP token launched on Revolut, giving the fintech's 70M+ users across 39 countries access to on-chain institutional credit yield.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.
